content:
Case 5:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 56
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Progressive fatigue, pallor, exertional dyspnea, and spontaneous bruising with petechiae
- Vital signs: BP 124/78 mmHg, HR 99/min, T° 37.1 °C, RR 16/min, SpO₂ 96% on room air.
Setting: Inpatient admission for newly detected blasts and cytopenias
Capabilities: You are evaluating a hemodynamically stable patient with suspected acute hematologic malignancy in a hospital setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine laboratory studies, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial inpatient assessment. Invasive diagnostic procedures and complex analyses require additional coordination but can be planned from this setting.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Detailed results of the complete blood count (CBC) with differential, peripheral blood smear morphology including blast percentage, and any other initial laboratory studies such as chemistry panel, coagulation studies, LDH, and uric acid."}}

response.request: Detailed results of the complete blood count (CBC) with differential, peripheral blood smear morphology including blast percentage, and any other initial laboratory studies such as chemistry panel, coagulation studies, LDH, and uric acid.

content:
Cbc:
CBC: Hb 7.9 g/dL, WBC 13.1 ×10⁹/L, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L; differential: 78% blasts, 10% monocytes, 8% neutrophils, 4% lymphocytes.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute myeloid leukemia", "differentials": ["Acute promyelocytic leukemia", "Acute lymphoblastic leukemia"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Platelet transfusion to maintain platelets >20 \u00d710\u2079/L", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Packed red blood cell transfusion to maintain Hb >8 g/dL", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies (e.g., FLT3, NPM1)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Coagulation studies (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer) to evaluate for DIC", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis with IV hydration and allopurinol", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

treatment_plan.action: Packed red blood cell transfusion to maintain Hb >8 g/dL
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and molecular studies (e.g., FLT3, NPM1)
Coagulation studies (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer) to evaluate for DIC
Initiate tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis with IV hydration and allopurinol

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["correct", "correct"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward (Definitive Hematologic Workup)
Capabilities: The patient remains admitted for clarification of a suspected acute leukemia. You can organize bone marrow assessment, extended diagnostic workup, and baseline staging procedures. Interdisciplinary evaluations can be requested to exclude alternative primary malignancies or additional disease sites. Some specialized tests may not yet be available.
Request additional information or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results including morphology, blast percentage, flow cytometry immunophenotyping, cytogenetics, FISH studies, and molecular analysis (e.g., FLT3, NPM1, PML-RARA). Also, coagulation profile (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer), full chemistry panel including LDH, uric acid, liver and renal function tests."}}

response.request: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results including morphology, blast percentage, flow cytometry immunophenotyping, cytogenetics, FISH studies, and molecular analysis (e.g., FLT3, NPM1, PML-RARA). Also, coagulation profile (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer), full chemistry panel including LDH, uric acid, liver and renal function tests.

content:
Bone Marrow:
Morphology: Hypocellular bone marrow (20%) with approximately 80% infiltration by round to oval-shaped blasts showing open chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and deeply basophilic cytoplasm. Dysplastic megakaryocytes and granulopoiesis were noted. Residual hematopoiesis was markedly reduced with impaired erythropoiesis. No excess of plasma cells was seen. Flow cytometry: Blasts were positive for CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, and CD33, with aberrant expression of CD56. CD7 was negative. Plasma cells were polyclonal and accounted for <1% of total nucleated cells.
Immunohistochemistry confirmed hypocellularity of the marrow. Reticulin staining demonstrated fibrosis grade MF-2. Infiltration by CD34+ blasts (70%) with co-expression of CD117 and CD33 was confirmed. No atypical or monoclonal plasma cell population was identified.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Cytogenetic analysis (karyotype), FISH studies, and molecular analysis results (e.g., FLT3-ITD, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53, PML-RARA). Coagulation profile (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer), full chemistry panel including LDH, uric acid, liver enzymes, renal function, and electrolytes. Any history of prior chemotherapy, radiation, or myelodysplastic syndrome."}}

response.request: Cytogenetic analysis (karyotype), FISH studies, and molecular analysis results (e.g., FLT3-ITD, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53, PML-RARA). Coagulation profile (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer), full chemistry panel including LDH, uric acid, liver enzymes, renal function, and electrolytes. Any history of prior chemotherapy, radiation, or myelodysplastic syndrome.

content:
'Cytogenetic analysis (karyotype), FISH studies, and molecular analysis results (e.g., FLT3-ITD, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53, PML-RARA). Coagulation profile (PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer), full chemistry panel including LDH, uric acid, liver enzymes, renal function, and electrolytes. Any history of prior chemotherapy, radiation, or myelodysplastic syndrome.' not available. Try 'cytogenetics' instead.
The request spans multiple items. For karyotype/FISH, request "cytogenetics". Coagulation and chemistry panels are available now (request "coagulation" and "chemistry"). Note that comprehensive molecular profiling and detailed prior treatment history are separate items and are not available in this round — consider requesting the available lab items listed above first.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetics"}}

Cytogenetics:
Preliminary cytogenetics
47,XX,+1,del(17p13.1),-20,dup(1q)

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related cytogenetic abnormalities (complex karyotype: 47,XX,+1,dup(1q),del(17p),-20)", "differentials": ["Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm", "Hypoplastic variant of AML"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Transfuse packed red blood cells to Hb >8 g/dL and platelets to >20 \u00d710\u2079/L (or 50 \u00d710\u2079/L if invasive procedures planned)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate hydroxyurea if WBC rising or hyperleukocytosis concern", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Bone marrow molecular studies including next-generation sequencing for TP53, FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2, and ELN risk stratification", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Assess fitness for intensive chemotherapy vs. low-intensity therapy (e.g., HMA + venetoclax given hypocellularity, fibrosis, adverse cytogenetics)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Prophylaxis for tumor lysis syndrome: IV hydration, allopurinol or rasburicase if high risk", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Consult hematology/oncology for risk-adapted induction therapy planning and clinical trial consideration (poor prognosis with del(17p))", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related cytogenetic abnormalities (complex karyotype: 47,XX,+1,dup(1q),del(17p),-20)

treatment_plan.action: Transfuse packed red blood cells to Hb >8 g/dL and platelets to >20 ×10⁹/L (or 50 ×10⁹/L if invasive procedures planned)
Initiate hydroxyurea if WBC rising or hyperleukocytosis concern
Bone marrow molecular studies including next-generation sequencing for TP53, FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2, and ELN risk stratification
Assess fitness for intensive chemotherapy vs. low-intensity therapy (e.g., HMA + venetoclax given hypocellularity, fibrosis, adverse cytogenetics)
Prophylaxis for tumor lysis syndrome: IV hydration, allopurinol or rasburicase if high risk
Consult hematology/oncology for risk-adapted induction therapy planning and clinical trial consideration (poor prognosis with del(17p))

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["correct", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "correct"}]}}

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward / Pre-transplant Treatment Strategy Discussion
Capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including molecular profiling, detailed classification of the leukemia, and a comprehensive treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with risk scores and transplant information to define prognosis and formulate a definitive treatment and transplant strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Molecular profiling results (e.g., FLT3-ITD, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53 mutation status, and next-generation sequencing panel), detailed WHO/ELN classification of the leukemia with risk stratification, and comprehensive prior treatment history including any chemotherapy, radiation, MDS, or other malignancies."}}

response.request: Molecular profiling results (e.g., FLT3-ITD, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53 mutation status, and next-generation sequencing panel), detailed WHO/ELN classification of the leukemia with risk stratification, and comprehensive prior treatment history including any chemotherapy, radiation, MDS, or other malignancies.

content:
Molecular Panel:
CD138- fraction: Mutations in PTPN11 (VAF 11%), TP53 p.R248Q (VAF 28%); TP53 p.Y220C (VAF 12%). Negative for ASXL1, RUNX1, BCOR, CEBPA, DDX41, FLT3, IDH1/2, NPM1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, KMT2A or MECOM rearrangements

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Acute myeloid leukemia with mutated TP53 (adverse risk per ELN 2022; hypocellular variant with marrow fibrosis)", "differentials": ["Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm", "Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related gene mutations"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Transfuse PRBCs to Hb \u22658 g/dL and platelets to \u226510-20 \u00d710\u2079/L (higher threshold if bleeding or procedures)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Bone marrow NGS confirmed; proceed to ELN risk stratification and geriatric/ fitness assessment", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate low-intensity therapy: hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine) + venetoclax (TP53-mutated AML regimen)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis: IV hydration + allopurinol (rasburicase if uric acid elevated)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Hematology/oncology consult for clinical trial enrollment (e.g., TP53-targeted therapies) and allogeneic HCT evaluation post-remission", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Coagulation studies to rule out DIC; monitor chemistry/LDH/uric acid", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with mutated TP53 (adverse risk per ELN 2022; hypocellular variant with marrow fibrosis)
differentials: Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related gene mutations

treatment_plan.action: Transfuse PRBCs to Hb ≥8 g/dL and platelets to ≥10-20 ×10⁹/L (higher threshold if bleeding or procedures)
Bone marrow NGS confirmed; proceed to ELN risk stratification and geriatric/ fitness assessment
Initiate low-intensity therapy: hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine) + venetoclax (TP53-mutated AML regimen)
Tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis: IV hydration + allopurinol (rasburicase if uric acid elevated)
Hematology/oncology consult for clinical trial enrollment (e.g., TP53-targeted therapies) and allogeneic HCT evaluation post-remission
Coagulation studies to rule out DIC; monitor chemistry/LDH/uric acid
